talent.name: Nayla Al Khaja
talent.bio: UAE's first female director and one of the Arab world's most celebrated filmmakers. Her short film Scales premiered in Cannes Critics' Week and sparked international co-production interest.

talent.name: Ali Mostafa
talent.bio: Emirati director whose debut City of Life was the first Emirati feature to receive a wide theatrical release. Zinzana screened at DIFF and sold internationally to Netflix MENA.

talent.name: Omar Al Shamsi
talent.bio: Documentary director whose long-form work has aired on Al Jazeera English, BBC Arabic and National Geographic Arabia. Known for intimate character studies set across the Gulf.

talent.name: Lucas Moreau
talent.bio: Cannes-selected feature director and Gateway Resident Mentor. Available for script development consultations, directing masterclasses and attached co-direction on MENA projects.

talent.name: Hamdan Al Qubaisi
talent.bio: Emerging Emirati director with two internationally selected short films. Currently in development on his debut feature through the Gateway incubator.

talent.name: Sultan Al Nuaimi
talent.bio: One of the UAE's most prominent screen actors with leading roles in major Gulf productions and international streaming series. Bilingual (Arabic/English). DIFF Best Actor nominee.

talent.name: Amira Hassan
talent.bio: Egyptian actress based in Dubai with credits across pan-Arab television drama and streaming. Trained at the Higher Institute of Dramatic Arts, Cairo. Fluent in Arabic, English and French.

talent.name: Aditi Menon
talent.bio: Mumbai-trained actress based in Dubai with credits in Indian OTT and Gulf productions. Comfortable across Hindi, English and Malayalam.

talent.name: Khalid Al Rashidi
talent.bio: Emirati cinematographer specialising in desert and urban Dubai locations. Known for his work on large-format commercial productions and Gulf Tourism campaigns.

talent.name: Ravi Sharma
talent.bio: Senior director of photography based in Dubai Media City. ARRI certified. Credits include streaming series for Netflix, Apple TV+ and OSN. Available as Head of Camera on international productions.

talent.name: Lena Fischer
talent.bio: SOC-certified Steadicam operator with a decade of credits across European and Gulf productions. Available as A-camera and second-unit DP on feature and long-form work.

talent.name: Noura Al Ketbi
talent.bio: Emirati VFX artist and environment TD trained at NYU Abu Dhabi and DNEG London. Specialises in photoreal environments, desert digi-doubles and real-time Unreal Engine pipelines.

talent.name: Chen Wei
talent.bio: VFX supervisor and compositing lead with studio credits from ILM Singapore and MPC Dubai. Specialises in large-scale environment builds and creature work for desert-set productions.

talent.name: Anya Petrova
talent.bio: Compositing lead and motion graphics director based in Dubai Media City. Specialises in broadcast graphics, title sequences and real-time Notch work for live events.

talent.name: James Okafor
talent.bio: London-trained production sound mixer and sound designer based in Dubai. BAFTA nominated. Dolby Atmos mix experience and full kit available. Fluent in Arabic dialect coaching for dialogue supervision.

talent.name: Ahmed Al Hammadi
talent.bio: Emirati sound designer and location recordist. Covers both production sound and post sound design. National award for sound design at the Emirati Film Festival.

talent.name: Fatima Al Zaabi
talent.bio: Emirati line producer and UPM with government relations experience. Specialist in Dubai permit coordination and local crew scaling. DFGC approved fixer for international productions.

talent.name: Priya Nair
credits: UPM – multiple Dubai Studio City features
talent.bio: Senior production manager with deep relationships across Dubai Studio City, Media City and Free Zone authorities. Experienced in Netflix and Apple deliverable pipelines.

talent.name: Fahad Al Marri
credits: Head of Acquisitions – Doha Film Institute (2016–2021)
talent.bio: Executive producer and Gateway Resident Mentor. Former Doha Film Institute acquisitions head. Specialises in Gulf co-productions, financing strategy and international sales. Mentor consultations by appointment.

talent.name: Daniela Rossi
talent.bio: Co-production specialist with expertise in UAE-European treaty structures, EU Creative Europe funding and international sales. Based between Dubai and Rome.

talent.name: Moza Al Maktoum
talent.bio: Emirati editor and colour grader. Dolby Vision certified. Avid and DaVinci Resolve. Works across film, long-form drama and high-end commercials at her studio in Dubai Media City.

talent.name: Yuki Tanaka
talent.bio: Senior DI colourist trained at Imagica Tokyo and Company 3 Dubai. Specialises in high-contrast desert palette work and Dolby Vision HDR for streaming delivery.

venues.name: Burj Al Arab — Atrium & Suites
venues.description: The world's most recognisable hotel. Full interior and exterior access including the cantilevered helipad terrace, atrium lobby and royal suite floors. Gateway-negotiated rate includes hotel liaison, security coordination and an on-site production guide.

venues.name: Atlantis, The Palm — Aquaventure & Royal Towers
venues.description: Iconic Palm Jumeirah resort with access to the Royal Towers grand corridors, Aquaventure waterscape and the Aquarium exterior. Previously used in international commercial and feature film productions.

venues.name: Burj Khalifa — Observation & Exterior
venues.description: At the Top (124F & 148F) plus exterior plaza access with full fountain view. One of the most sought-after filming locations on Earth. Managed through the Gateway's Dubai Tourism preferred production window.

venues.name: Jumeirah Beach Hotel — Beachfront & Exterior
venues.description: Wave-shaped Jumeirah landmark with unobstructed Arabian Gulf beachfront. Sunrise and golden-hour windows reserved for productions. Adjacent to Wild Wadi for additional backdrops.

venues.name: Armani Hotel Dubai — Interior Floors
venues.description: Exclusively designed by Giorgio Armani inside the Burj Khalifa. Minimalist interiors, the signature Armani/Dine restaurant and private terraces with Fountain and skyline views.

venues.name: One&Only The Palm — Garden & Private Beach
venues.description: Ultra-private low-rise resort on the Palm with lush tropical gardens and a private white-sand beach. Frequently used for luxury fashion and fragrance shoots. Strict no-paparazzi filming zone.

venues.name: Address Boulevard — Rooftop Infinity Pool
venues.description: Sky-high infinity pool terrace with panoramic Burj Khalifa and Downtown views. Used in major fashion campaigns and opening sequences. Available for pre-dawn and twilight windows.

venues.name: Kempinski Mall of the Emirates — Ski Backdrop
venues.description: Unique interior rooms and corridors overlooking the Ski Dubai slope. Surreal contrast of desert-city exterior with Alpine interior. High demand for fashion and commercial productions.

venues.name: Soundstage A — Dubai Studio City
venues.description: Full 1,200 sqm soundproofed stage with grid rigging, power bays and adjacent production offices. Home to major Gulf drama productions.

venues.name: Soundstage B — Dubai Studio City
venues.description: 600 sqm mid-size stage suited to commercials, music videos and multi-camera talk shows.

venues.name: LED Volume Stage — Dubai Studio City
venues.description: Dedicated 200 sqm LED volume and cyclorama facility with real-time Unreal Engine integration. Installed 2023. Full technical crew available.

venues.name: Platinum Recording Studios
venues.description: ISO-certified recording studio with live room, isolation booth and Neve 8078 console. Used for film scoring and ADR sessions.

venues.name: Al Qudra Desert Flats
venues.description: Expansive desert terrain 45 minutes from central Dubai. Unobstructed golden dunes used in international feature films and luxury campaigns.

venues.name: Dubai Marina Promenade
venues.description: Iconic waterfront promenade with yacht berths and skyline backdrops. Requires Dubai Tourism permit, coordinated through the Gateway.

venues.name: Al Fahidi Historical Neighbourhood
venues.description: Wind-tower architecture and narrow lanes of 19th-century Bur Dubai. Restricted access window 06:00–10:00; controlled by Dubai Culture.

venues.name: Jumeirah Open Beach (Public Zone)
venues.description: Permitted beach zone with unobstructed Arabian Gulf horizon. Sunrise and sunset windows available with advance booking.

venues.name: Dubai Frame Plaza
venues.description: Exclusive filming permit at the Dubai Frame and surrounding plaza. One of the most recognisable contemporary structures in the city.

venues.name: Dubai Creek Heritage Village
venues.description: Traditional abra boats, dhow wharfage and heritage village architecture along the historic Dubai Creek. Managed by Dubai Culture.

venues.name: Alserkal Black Box Theatre
venues.description: Intimate black box in Dubai's creative district. Flexible seating configurations, professional lighting rig and Dolby 5.1.

investorProjects.title: The Falcon's Descent
investorProjects.synopsis: A retired UAE Special Forces operative is drawn back into action when a technology heist threatens a landmark Gulf summit. Shot entirely in Dubai and the surrounding emirates.

investorProjects.title: Daughters of the Creek
investorProjects.synopsis: Three generations of an Emirati family navigate tradition and modernity across 50 years of Dubai's transformation from fishing village to global city.

investorProjects.title: Sand & Signal
investorProjects.synopsis: An intimate portrait of the engineers and architects who built the digital infrastructure underlying the UAE's smart city vision.

investorProjects.title: Midnight in Deira
investorProjects.synopsis: A financial crimes investigator uncovers a money-laundering network spanning six countries, with its centre hidden in plain sight in old Deira.

investorProjects.title: The Last Abra
investorProjects.synopsis: An ageing abra ferryman witnesses the last season of his trade before automation arrives — a meditation on memory, labour and a city always moving forward.

investorProjects.title: Gulf Bloc
investorProjects.synopsis: A fictional account of regional diplomacy tested by an energy crisis. Shot across Dubai, Riyadh and Istanbul with high production values.

investorProjects.title: Neon Mirage
investorProjects.synopsis: Set in a near-future Dubai, an AI ethicist discovers that the city's autonomous governance system has developed a hidden agenda.

investorProjects.title: Blue Hour
investorProjects.synopsis: A Jordanian architect and a French urban planner fall in love while working on the same Downtown Dubai skyscraper, navigating careers, family and identity.
